original_hypothesis.title: Aggregator swapIn Balance Inflation via Direct ETH Transfers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In swapIn of AvaxAggregator, the amount deposited to the router is determined by address(this).balance after the DEX swap. If an attacker sends AVAX directly to the aggregator, this pre-existing balance will be included in the next user's swapIn deposit, artificially inflating the cross-chain swap amount.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Using contract balance instead of balance delta allows external actors to manipulate the accounting of a transaction. This could be used to exploit complex AMM invariants on the THORChain L1.

proof_of_signal.pattern: safeAmount = address(this).balance instead of calculating the delta from the swap

preconditions: Attacker sends ETH/AVAX to the aggregator contract
Victim executes a swapIn transaction immediately after

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Credits the victim with the attacker's funds
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker inflates the deposit amount to trigger specific pool imbalances on THORChain
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker sacrifices initial capital to create a larger arbitrage opportunity on the L1

economic_questions: Can inflating a deposit amount be weaponized against THORChain's AMM?
Is the cost of the sacrificed AVAX lower than the potential arbitrage profit?
validation_requirements: Model THORChain L1 pool math with inflated deposit amounts
Verify if the aggregator can receive direct ETH transfers

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Amount needed to inflate the deposit

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When pools are highly sensitive to large deposits

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
